mechanism: The tiered lag structure (energy → food → services) means first-round effects arrive sequentially across sectors, creating an inflation breadth signal that can be confused with genuine second-round broadening. This may cause central banks to over-tighten.
regime_dependence: Most likely to create confusion during large commodity shocks with long subsequent services lags (6-36 months). Less confusing when shocks are small and services inflation remains stable throughout.
